changes_summary:
Major revisions from v1:

**Phase 3 completely rewritten** — useTimelineData is NOT a pure pass-through. It owns pruneSelectionRef, onSaveSuccessRef→setRenderDirty wiring, and a useLayoutEffect bridge. The revised plan extracts types into useTimelineData.types.ts first (covering all 13+ dependents), then inlines the orchestration into useTimelineState with explicit handling of each piece.

**Phase 5 completely rewritten** — useDerivedTimeline is NOT just a signature cache. It computes selectedClip, selectedTrack, selectedClipHasPredecessor, compositionSize, renderMetadata, and trackScaleMap. It's also called twice (once with selectedClipId, once with primaryClipId for multi-select). The revised plan preserves the hook and only simplifies the signature cache mechanism.

**Phase 6 (audio) completely rewritten** — The repo uses Remotion Sequence + Audio exclusively with no custom Web Audio buffers. The revised plan acknowledges the key={id-at} pattern is actually correct for Remotion's model, and only makes a targeted improvement (include all audio-affecting properties in the key) with documentation.

**Phase 7 (context split) dropped entirely** — The repo already has a deliberate 3-context split (Editor/Chrome/Playback). Proposing a different 4-context split was scope creep and conflicted with existing architecture.

**Poll gating corrected** — pendingOpsRef is used by both drag sessions AND asset operations. The isIdle formula explicitly includes pendingOpsRef > 0. No caller behavior changes.

**createTrackAndMoveClip preserved** — Intentionally skips overlap resolution (new track = no neighbors). Under unified applyEdit it uses type: 'config' path, preserving existing semantics.

**Test command fixed** — Changed from workspace-root to cd reigh-app first.

**Success criteria re-prioritized** — useTimelineSave.ts line count demoted from must to should. AudioTrack criterion made concrete and verifiable.
questions: Phase 1 Step 3: The isIdle formula includes pendingOpsRef === 0 alongside editSeq/savedSeq/isSaving checks. Should we add any additional idle signals (e.g., no active resize), or are the existing pendingOpsRef increments in useClipDrag sufficient to cover all interactive operations?

Phase 3 Step 9: When inlining useTimelineData's orchestration into useTimelineState, the latter grows from ~435 lines. Should we extract the orchestration wiring into a small useTimelineOrchestration helper, or is having it inline in useTimelineState acceptable given it's the top-level compositor?

success_criteria.criterion: All 20 existing video-editor test files pass after each phase
Full app test suite passes after final phase
TypeScript compilation (tsc --noEmit) succeeds after each phase
Single applyEdit entry point replaces applyTimelineEdit + applyResolvedConfigEdit in all internal hook callers
Poll rejection defaults to reject; polls only accepted when system is idle (isIdle whitelist check)
resolveGroupOverlaps returns metaPatches (fixes the documented TODO at resolve-overlaps.ts:248)
useTimelineData.ts is deleted; all type re-exports preserved in useTimelineData.types.ts
pendingOpsRef behavior unchanged — drag and asset operations both still increment/decrement, existing tests pass
createTrackAndMoveClip preserves current behavior (config path, no overlap resolution)
useTimelineSave.ts reduced to thin composer (<150 lines)
No single hook file exceeds ~400 lines
Hook hierarchy is max 2 layers deep (useTimelineState -> focused sub-hooks)
New tests added for resolveGroupOverlaps meta patch generation
Each phase is independently committable (no half-finished intermediate states)
AudioTrack key includes all audio-affecting properties (at, from, to, speed) with explanatory comment
Remotion preview does not flicker or remount unnecessarily during editing
Manual smoke test: edit, undo, redo, save, poll, conflict retry all work

assumptions: Asset uploads (useAssetOperations) and drag sessions (useClipDrag) both use pendingOpsRef. The isIdle derivation includes pendingOpsRef === 0, so both are covered without changing any caller behavior.
createTrackAndMoveClip intentionally skips overlap resolution because a new track has no neighbors to overlap with. This is preserved in the unified API by routing it through the config path.
useDerivedTimeline's signature cache may become unnecessary once poll rejection is default-reject, but we will verify empirically before simplifying. If Remotion flickers, we revert.
UseTimelineDataResult can be extracted to a separate types module and all 13+ importers updated in a single safe step before the hook itself is deleted.
The vitest config lives at reigh-app/config/testing/vitest.config.ts and all test commands must run from the reigh-app/ directory.
